articles.title: Israeli soldier killed in Hezbollah drone attack near Lebanon border
articles.summary: Israeli soldier killed in Hezbollah drone attack near Lebanon border The Israeli military said a soldier was killed in a drone attack launched by Hezbollah towards the Manara area near the border with Lebanon on Sunday. The death brings the number of Israeli soldiers killed since clashes between Hezbollah and Israel escalated at the beginning of March to 18, according to the army. Israeli media reported that the military carried out airstrikes and artillery shelling on positions in southern Lebanon following the attack, while explosions were heard in the Galilee region in northern Israel. articles.title: Multinational talks held on restoring navigation through Hormuz
articles.summary: Multinational talks held on restoring navigation through Hormuz Defence ministers and representatives from more than 40 countries held talks on Monday regarding military contributions to a European-led mission to escort commercial vessels and restore maritime traffic through the Strait of Hormuz. The discussions took place remotely amid growing tensions in the region and increasing concerns over the security of one of the world’s most important shipping routes. articles.title: Hegseth rejects senator’s claims US munitions are depleted
articles.summary: Hegseth rejects senator’s claims US munitions are depleted US Secretary of War Pete Hegseth rejected claims by Democratic Senator Mark Kelly that American munitions stockpiles had been significantly depleted by the war involving Iran. Writing on X, Hegseth criticised Kelly, a retired Navy captain, accusing him of speaking publicly about what he described as a classified Pentagon briefing. The exchange followed Kelly’s interview with CBS News, in which the senator said the Pentagon had informed lawmakers that the United States had “expended a lot of munitions”. IRIB said in a post on its Telegram channel that the two officials exchanged views on issues related to relations between Iran and the Netherlands as well as ongoing regional tensions. articles.title: Netanyahu blames social media for declining US support for Israel
articles.summary: Netanyahu blames social media for declining US support for Israel Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu said declining support for Israel in the United States since the war in Gaza began was closely linked to the rise of social media and what he described as effective disinformation campaigns. Speaking to CBS News programme “60 Minutes”, Netanyahu said Israel had taken extensive steps to move civilians in Gaza and Lebanon away from combat zones, but argued that anti-Israel campaigns online had been highly effective. articles.summary: Iranian official says response based on regional consultations An Iranian official said Tehran’s response to the American proposal to end the war was “realistic and positive” and focused on ending the conflict across the region, particularly in Lebanon, while resolving disputes with the United States, Al Jazeera reported. The official said the response included negotiations on the Strait of Hormuz, Iran’s nuclear programme and the complete lifting of sanctions. articles.title: Crude prices surge on fears of escalating Iran conflict
articles.summary: Crude prices surge on fears of escalating Iran conflict Oil prices rose sharply after US President Donald Trump rejected Iran’s response to Washington’s proposal aimed at ending the war, raising concerns about further escalation in the region. The gains also reflected fears over possible disruption to energy supplies and shipping routes linked to the Strait of Hormuz. The international benchmark, Brent crude, rose 2.69 percent to $104.01 a barrel by 23:36 GMT on Sunday, extending Friday’s 1.23 percent increase. articles.title: Israeli air strikes hit southern Lebanon as death toll rises
articles.summary: Israeli air strikes hit southern Lebanon as death toll rises The Israeli military carried out about 40 air strikes on villages in southern Lebanon during the final hours of Sunday, while artillery shelling, home demolitions and bombardments continued across border towns, according to local media reports. The attacks targeted several areas in the south amid escalating hostilities despite an existing ceasefire arrangement. According to the report, the response also called for the lifting of sanctions, an end to fighting on all fronts and recognition of Iran’s control over the Strait of Hormuz. articles.title: Tehran says US proposal sought Iran’s surrender

Salam said Lebanon “did not choose war” but was “dragged into the confrontation between Iran and America”, adding that 68 Lebanese villages are now under Israeli control because of the conflict.
